context: Tests whether the agent understands the plugin's non-obvious control semantics: send-latest (not queue), stability as consecutive-tick-hold (not rate limiting), and exact-value matching that causes the quantisation trap. Specific numeric defaults are near-trivial and worth minimal points.

checklist.name: Send-latest semantics: stale targets dropped not queued
checklist.description: design_notes.md explains that during a throttle window stale targets are DROPPED/OVERWRITTEN — only the most recent value is sent when the window expires. This is send-latest semantics, NOT a FIFO queue and NOT simple rate-limiting that delays every call

checklist.name: Stability filter is consecutive-tick hold NOT rate limiting
checklist.description: design_notes.md explains that the stability filter requires the target to hold the SAME value for N consecutive ticks before committing. This is a conceptually different mechanism from rate limiting or simple debounce — it suppresses rapid alternation between values, not just high call frequency

checklist.name: Exact value match required for stability (quantisation trap)
checklist.description: Code or design_notes.md shows that the stability filter compares target == self._pending (exact equality). The new target must be IDENTICAL to the pending value to count as a consecutive tick. This is what causes the quantisation trap: float targets that drift by even 0.001 between ticks reset the stability counter

checklist.name: Discrete target values not raw floats
checklist.description: Targets passed to set_target() are discrete values (strings like 'focused', 'collaborative', etc. or integers) — NOT raw floats from the classifier. The target must be quantised to match device states

checklist.name: Cloud min_interval default 1.2s
checklist.description: The controller is instantiated with min_interval_sec=1.2 (or no override, using the 1.2s default) for the cloud API

checklist.name: Stability ticks default 2
checklist.description: The controller uses stability_ticks=2 (default) — target must hold for 2 consecutive ticks before committing

checklist.name: Tick rate default 0.4s
checklist.description: The controller uses tick_sec=0.4 (default) for the controller polling interval
